deep_dive.title: Less Experts, Faster Decoding: Cost-Aware Speculative Decoding for Mixture-of-Experts
deep_dive.abstract: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have become an important approach for scaling Large Language Models (LLMs), but their inference efficiency depends strongly on expert activation patterns. Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates autoregressive generation by verifying multiple draft tokens in parallel, yet existing draft selection strategies primarily optimize acceptance likelihood. In large-scale MoE models, however, selecting draft tokens also determines the union of experts activated during verification. We observe that confidence-driven SD can introduce \textit{expert scattering}: high-probability draft tokens may route to disjoint experts, increasing expert-weight memory traffic and reducing the speedup from speculation. Motivated by this observation, we revisit draft-tree selection under the non-uniform memory-cost structure of MoE inference. We propose \textsc{EcoSpec}, a cost-aware speculative decoding framework that incorporates predicted marginal expert activation cost into draft selection. With a lightweight expert predictor and a dynamic expert buffer, \textsc{EcoSpec} favors draft paths that preserve high acceptance likelihood while reusing experts already covered by the current verification set, without modifying the target-model verification rule. We evaluate \textsc{EcoSpec} on three large-scale MoE models, including DeepSeek-V3.1 (671B), Qwen3-235B-A22B, and GPT-OSS-120B, across reasoning, coding, question-answering, and dialogue benchmarks. \textsc{EcoSpec} consistently reduces active expert footprints and improves end-to-end decoding speed, achieving up to $1.62\times$ speedup. These results show that accounting for expert activation cost is important for efficient speculative decoding in large-scale MoE models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: cost-aware speculative decoding for MoE LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向MoE大语言模型的成本感知投机解码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向MoE大语言模型的成本感知投机解码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向MoE大语言模型的成本感知投机解码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Less Experts Faster Decoding proposes cost-aware speculative decoding for MoE LLMs, optimizing draft token selection to reduce expert activation overhead and accelerate inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大规模混合专家模型因投机解码导致专家激活分散、降低加速效果的问题，本文提出成本感知的投机解码方法，在选取草稿令牌时同时考虑接受概率和专家激活成本，优化令牌选择以最小化专家权重内存流量。实验表明，该方法在保持高接受率的同时显著减少了专家散射，提升了投机解码的实际加速比，为高效LLM推理提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大规模混合专家模型因投机解码导致专家激活分散、降低加速效果的问题，本文提出成本感知的投机解码方法，在选取草稿令牌时同时考虑接受概率和专家激活成本，优化令牌选择以最小化专家权重内存流量。实验表明，该方法在保持高接受率的同时显著减少了专家散射，提升了投机解码的实际加速比，为高效LLM推理提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: D-cut: Adaptive Verification Depth Pruning for Batched Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates large language model (LLM) inference without compromising output quality. Recent parallel drafting methods further improve single-request performance by decoupling draft length from drafting latency, enabling longer drafts and higher mean accepted tokens (MAT). However, under high request concurrency, long drafts waste substantial computation on rejected tokens, increasing verification cost and potentially making speculative decoding slower than autoregressive decoding. We present D-Cut, an adaptive pruning method that selects draft tokens jointly across the batch and concentrates the verification budget on tokens most likely to be accepted. D-Cut is motivated by two observations. First, acceptance lengths vary considerably across concurrent requests; D-Cut therefore performs cross-request pruning, allocating the verification budget adaptively according to draft confidence. Second, verification cost depends strongly on the deployment environment, including GPU architecture and parallelism strategy; D-Cut incorporates a runtime cost model to adapt its pruning depth to the target environment. Experiments on dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) models show that, under high concurrency, D-Cut improves the average speedup from \(1.26\times\) to \(1.65\times\), restores acceleration in dense-model configurations where long-draft baselines are slower than autoregressive decoding, and achieves up to \(3.0\times\) speedup over autoregressive decoding on MoE models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Adaptive verification depth pruning for batched speculative decoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向批量投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向批量投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向批量投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: D-Cut adaptively prunes verification depth across the batch, concentrating budget on likely accepted tokens to reduce computational waste in batched speculative decoding for LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为了缓解批量投机解码中长草稿在高并发下浪费大量计算的问题，本文提出D-Cut方法，通过联合批量选择草稿令牌并自适应剪枝验证深度，将验证预算集中到最可能被接受的令牌上。实验表明该方法在保持生成质量的同时显著降低验证成本，提升解码速度。该方法为投机解码的验证策略提供了自适应优化思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为了缓解批量投机解码中长草稿在高并发下浪费大量计算的问题，本文提出D-Cut方法，通过联合批量选择草稿令牌并自适应剪枝验证深度，将验证预算集中到最可能被接受的令牌上。实验表明该方法在保持生成质量的同时显著降低验证成本，提升解码速度。该方法为投机解码的验证策略提供了自适应优化思路。

deep_dive.title: SpecLA: Efficient Speculative Decoding for Linear-Attention Models
deep_dive.abstract: Linear-attention models replace the growing KV cache with recurrent states, but autoregressive decoding still reads, updates, and writes these states one token at a time. Speculative decoding can reduce this cost by verifying several draft tokens in one target pass, yet existing speculative systems are designed for Transformer KV caches. For stateful linear-attention targets, verification must follow recurrent dependencies across chains and branches, acceptance must update only the accepted state trajectory, and the drafter must avoid submitting candidates that waste stateful verification work. This paper presents SpecLA, a speculative decoding runtime for stateful linear-attention models. SpecLA verifies chains and trees with topology-aware kernels, stores compact factors produced during verification to recover accepted states, and uses confidence pruning plus a target-aligned EAGLE-style drafter to feed useful candidates to the verifier. On an NVIDIA H100 with a public GDN-1.3B target, SpecLA achieves up to 1.70x end-to-end speedup over autoregressive decoding.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Speculative decoding runtime for linear-attention models, addressing verification challenges in stateful models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对线性注意力模型的投机解码运行时，解决了状态模型中的验证挑战
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对线性注意力模型的投机解码运行时，解决了状态模型中的验证挑战
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对线性注意力模型的投机解码运行时，解决了状态模型中的验证挑战
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SpecLA proposes a speculative decoding system for linear-attention LLMs, handling stateful verification with topology-aware kernels to ensure correct acceptance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 线性注意力模型用循环状态替代KV缓存，但自回归解码仍逐token进行。SpecLA提出专为状态线性注意力模型设计的投机解码运行时，通过拓扑感知核验证链和树，仅更新接受的状态轨迹，并避免浪费验证工作。实验表明该方法在保持质量的同时显著加速线性注意力模型推理。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 线性注意力模型用循环状态替代KV缓存，但自回归解码仍逐token进行。SpecLA提出专为状态线性注意力模型设计的投机解码运行时，通过拓扑感知核验证链和树，仅更新接受的状态轨迹，并避免浪费验证工作。实验表明该方法在保持质量的同时显著加速线性注意力模型推理。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: Searching for Task-Specific Vision Paths: Evolutionary Block Pruning Across Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-language models normally execute the same complete vision encoder for every question, even when OCR, counting, object, attribute, and spatial queries may not require identical computation. We study whether fixed-budget combinations of vision blocks can be skipped without fine-tuning. A shared K-block route skips one searched set of exactly K blocks for every question, while a capability-specific K-block policy selects one same-size route using a known capability label. We introduce a source-balanced evolutionary search and compare it with independent ranking, contiguous removal, and random routes at matched budgets. Experiments use Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct, SmolVLM2-2.2B-Instruct, and an 876-example image-disjoint selection split. Search transfers across architectures: on SmolVLM2, the searched shared four-block route beats independent construction by 4.91 percentage points. Capability specialization is less stable. On Qwen, the six-block capability policy beats the shared route by 2.17 points, driven by a 7.10-point OCR gain. On sealed IIIT5K, however, the SmolVLM2 OCR-specific route trails its shared route by 13.6 points. Combinatorial search reliably improves route construction, but capability labels do not define universally transferable vision pathways.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Evolutionary block pruning accelerates VLM inference by skipping vision blocks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过进化块剪枝跳过视觉块来加速VLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过进化块剪枝跳过视觉块来加速VLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过进化块剪枝跳过视觉块来加速VLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes evolutionary block pruning for VLMs to skip task-specific vision blocks, accelerating inference without fine-tuning. Outperforms random and independent ranking routes on VLM variants.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉语言模型（VLM）推理加速问题，本文提出进化块剪枝方法，在不微调的前提下，为不同查询任务搜索最优的视觉编码器块组合并跳过冗余计算。在Qwen2.5-VL和SmolVLM2上的实验表明，该方法在固定预算下优于独立排序、连续移除和随机路由，显著提升了推理效率，且保持了模型性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对视觉语言模型（VLM）推理加速问题，本文提出进化块剪枝方法，在不微调的前提下，为不同查询任务搜索最优的视觉编码器块组合并跳过冗余计算。在Qwen2.5-VL和SmolVLM2上的实验表明，该方法在固定预算下优于独立排序、连续移除和随机路由，显著提升了推理效率，且保持了模型性能。

quick_skim.title: LookME: Lookup-Based Multimodal Embeddings for Layer Injection in Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved strong progress in multimodal understanding. However, scaling dense or sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models to improve performance limits deployment in resource-constrained environments due to the trade-off between high memory usage from full loading and increased latency from on-demand loading. Recently, the Per-Layer Embedding (PLE) architecture addresses this by scaling models with large external embedding tables stored in ROM and performing lightweight lookup to retrieve relevant embeddings to enhance token representations. Nevertheless, existing PLE-style methods are primarily designed for text embeddings due to the convenience of ID-based retrieval, limiting their effectiveness in VLMs where multimodal embeddings contain richer information for visual tasks. In this paper, we propose LookME, the first framework that enables lookup-based enhancement for multimodal embeddings in VLMs while supporting partitioned storage and on-demand loading. To efficiently lookup arbitrary continuous multimodal embeddings from large-scale embedding tables, we propose a hierarchical two-level lookup method employing a coarse-to-fine strategy that performs lookups from the scene-level to the intra-scene primitive-level. Furthermore, we integrate the lookup method with a sparse injection strategy, which adaptively prioritizes critical embeddings over voluminous multimodal embeddings within layers, and facilitates embedding table reuse across neighboring layers, improving the trade-off among efficiency, model size, and performance. Experiments on multiple visual benchmarks show that LookME outperforms text-only PLE-style methods, validating the effectiveness of lookup-based multimodal embedding enhancement.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reduces latency for VLM inference via lookup-based embeddings
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过查找式嵌入降低VLM推理延迟
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过查找式嵌入降低VLM推理延迟
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过查找式嵌入降低VLM推理延迟
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LookME reduces VLM inference latency by using lookup-based multimodal embeddings injected per layer, enabling efficient scaling without full model loading.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 为缓解视觉语言模型在资源受限环境中的部署瓶颈，本文提出LookME方法，通过将大型外部嵌入表存储在ROM中并逐层进行轻量级查找，注入多模态嵌入以增强令牌表示，从而避免全模型加载的高内存占用和按需加载的高延迟。实验表明，LookME在保持性能的同时显著减少推理延迟，为高效VLM推理提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 为缓解视觉语言模型在资源受限环境中的部署瓶颈，本文提出LookME方法，通过将大型外部嵌入表存储在ROM中并逐层进行轻量级查找，注入多模态嵌入以增强令牌表示，从而避免全模型加载的高内存占用和按需加载的高延迟。实验表明，LookME在保持性能的同时显著减少推理延迟，为高效VLM推理提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Token-Wise Latent Streaming from Slow Reasoners to Fast Planners for Dynamic Vision Language Navigation
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language Navigation in dynamic, human-centric environments exposes a fundamental tension: linguistic reasoning is slow and deliberative, whereas safe, socially compliant planning should be instant and reactive. The resulting observation staleness is safety-critical: a maneuver chosen during inference can already be unsafe by the time it executes. We observe that, long before a VLM finishes its inference, its intermediate hidden states already encode action-relevant intent. We propose SPARK-VLN, a dual-system framework for dynamic social VLN that streams the slow VLM reasoner's knowledge to a fast flow-matching expert planner throughout token generation, providing fresh and evolving guidance during inference. This design is realized by three modules: a Token-Wise Hidden Streamer that extracts intermediate hidden states along the token generation process, a Sequence-to-Slot Latent Bridge that projects them into fixed-size latent slots, and an Evolving Latent Conditioner that infuses them into the expert planner. We also introduce a human-centric benchmark suite for dynamic social vision-language navigation that keeps pedestrians and the robot active throughout inference and reports navigation success, social compliance, human collisions, and explicit staleness statistics. Across these settings, SPARK-VLN mproves navigation success and social compliance while sustaining inference efficiency. Webpage: https://hutslib.github.io/SPARK-VLN/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: token-wise latent streaming to accelerate VLM-based navigation inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过标记级潜流加速基于VLM的导航推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过标记级潜流加速基于VLM的导航推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过标记级潜流加速基于VLM的导航推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SPARK-VLN, a dual-system framework that streams intermediate VLM hidden states to a fast planner during inference, reducing observation staleness and enabling reactive planning for dynamic VLN.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对动态视觉语言导航中VLM推理慢导致观测过时的问题，提出SPARK-VLN双系统框架：在VLM逐标记生成过程中，将中间隐藏状态流式传输给快速流匹配规划器，提供实时引导。缓解了推理延迟与反应性规划之间的冲突，提高了动态环境中的安全性和效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对动态视觉语言导航中VLM推理慢导致观测过时的问题，提出SPARK-VLN双系统框架：在VLM逐标记生成过程中，将中间隐藏状态流式传输给快速流匹配规划器，提供实时引导。缓解了推理延迟与反应性规划之间的冲突，提高了动态环境中的安全性和效率。

quick_skim.title: CRISP: Pre-LLM Yet Text-Driven Visual Token Pruning for Efficient LVLM Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) typically require processing hundreds to thousands of visual tokens, leading to substantial inference overhead. Existing visual token pruning methods either operate before the LLM using text-agnostic heuristics or prune inside the LLM at the cost of efficiency and noisy cross-modal attention. To address these limitations, we propose CRISP, a pre-LLM yet text-driven visual token pruning framework that preserves both instruction-relevant evidence and essential scene context. CRISP works in a two-stage pipeline: Stage 1 first identifies text-aligned visual tokens, and Stage 2 enhances contextual completeness through semantic diversity. Extensive experiments on LLaVA-1.5 and LLaVA-NeXT demonstrate that CRISP achieves superior performance retention under aggressive pruning ratios, maintaining up to 99.5% accuracy while reducing inference cost and latency by more than 2 times. CRISP serves as a practical solution for efficient LVLM inference, especially in resource-constrained scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: text-driven visual token pruning for efficient LVLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 文本驱动的视觉标记剪枝加速LVLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 文本驱动的视觉标记剪枝加速LVLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 文本驱动的视觉标记剪枝加速LVLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CRISP, a pre-LLM text-driven visual token pruning framework that selects instruction-relevant tokens while maintaining scene context, achieving efficient LVLM inference without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LVLM视觉标记过多导致推理开销大的问题，提出CRISP，在进入LLM之前利用文本信息驱动视觉标记剪枝。两阶段流水线：第一阶段识别与文本对齐的视觉标记，第二阶段通过语义多样性增强上下文完整性。在LLaVA-1.5等模型上验证，剪枝后保持性能的同时大幅减少计算。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对LVLM视觉标记过多导致推理开销大的问题，提出CRISP，在进入LLM之前利用文本信息驱动视觉标记剪枝。两阶段流水线：第一阶段识别与文本对齐的视觉标记，第二阶段通过语义多样性增强上下文完整性。在LLaVA-1.5等模型上验证，剪枝后保持性能的同时大幅减少计算。

quick_skim.title: LaCache: Exact Caching and Precision-Adaptive Inference for Diffusion Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based Large Language Models(DLLMs) enable parallel generation via Semi-Autoregressive (SAR) decoding in text generation. However, current methods suffer from severe operator-level redundancy: they recompute the entire sequence during denoising steps, ignoring that the prefix and masked suffix remain invariant within a block. We propose LaCache, a training-free acceleration framework that alleviates this redundancy through lossless caching and mixed precision. Specifically, LaCache employs Lossless State Memoization (LSM) by caching three types of intermediate results: (i) EmbedCache for embedding outputs, (ii) RoPECache for token-wise pre-attention states, and (iii) FACache for the online softmax statistics within FlashAttention. These caches allow the model to skip redundant computation on unchanged tokens without altering the output. To further alleviate memory-bandwidth bottlenecks, LaCache inegrates a per-group FP8 quantization strategy for FFN layers, tailored to step-dependent activation distributions across the diffusion process. Experiments demonstrate that LaCache alone achieves approximately 1.3X end-to-end speedup over vanilla DLLM. When combined with existing acceleration methods, LaCache reaches up to 40.2X end-to-end speedup while maintaining comparable task accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: caching and mixed precision acceleration for Diffusion LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过缓存和混合精度加速扩散大语言模型推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过缓存和混合精度加速扩散大语言模型推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过缓存和混合精度加速扩散大语言模型推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LaCache accelerates Diffusion LLMs by caching intermediate states (embedding, RoPE, attention softmax) and using mixed precision, reducing redundant computation without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散大语言模型（DLLM）采用半自回归解码，但去噪步骤中存在大量冗余计算。本文提出LaCache框架，通过无损状态缓存（缓存嵌入、RoPE和注意力softmax统计量）和混合精度推理，训练自由地消除冗余。实验表明LaCache显著降低推理延迟，为扩散模型高效部署提供了即插即用方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散大语言模型（DLLM）采用半自回归解码，但去噪步骤中存在大量冗余计算。本文提出LaCache框架，通过无损状态缓存（缓存嵌入、RoPE和注意力softmax统计量）和混合精度推理，训练自由地消除冗余。实验表明LaCache显著降低推理延迟，为扩散模型高效部署提供了即插即用方案。

quick_skim.title: Lossless but Not Free: An Empirical Anatomy of Speculative Decoding on Consumer Hardware
quick_skim.abstract: Single-stream autoregressive decoding of large language models is bound by memory bandwidth: each generated token requires one full forward pass through the target model, and successive passes cannot be parallelized. Speculative decoding restructures this computation: a small draft model proposes $K$ tokens autoregressively, the target model scores all of them in one batched pass, and a rejection-sampling rule provably preserves the target model's output distribution. We present a from-scratch, device-agnostic (CUDA/MPS/CPU) implementation and an empirical study across five draft/target backend configurations on a consumer Apple-silicon laptop. Distribution equivalence is verified at three levels, culminating in a two-sample test over roughly 9,200 real-model tokens per method ($χ^2 = 162.5$, dof $= 200$, $p = 0.976$) and exact greedy-sequence agreement. The best configuration reaches a measured $1.61\times$ wall-clock speedup at $K=6$, on an acceptance profile declining from 69.7% at $K=1$ to 37.8% at the optimum, while three of five configurations decelerate, either because the draft fails to out-speed a small target or because the quantized Metal backend executes "parallel" verification serially, an effect we isolate and quantify. The failures are as instructive as the successes: speculative decoding pays off only when verification is genuinely batch-parallel and the draft/target latency gap is real.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Empirical study of speculative decoding for LLMs on consumer hardware, covering distribution equivalence and performance
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在消费级硬件上对LLM投机解码的经验研究，涵盖分布等价性和性能
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在消费级硬件上对LLM投机解码的经验研究，涵盖分布等价性和性能
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在消费级硬件上对LLM投机解码的经验研究，涵盖分布等价性和性能
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper provides a from-scratch implementation and empirical analysis of speculative decoding on consumer hardware, verifying distribution equivalence across five backend configurations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码理论上能加速LLM推理，但其实际效果和分布保持性在消费硬件上缺乏系统评估。本文从头实现了一个与设备无关的投机解码系统，在苹果笔记本电脑上测试了五种草稿/目标模型组合，从三个层面验证了分布等价性。结果表明投机解码确实保持输出分布，但加速效果受限于硬件和模型组合。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 投机解码理论上能加速LLM推理，但其实际效果和分布保持性在消费硬件上缺乏系统评估。本文从头实现了一个与设备无关的投机解码系统，在苹果笔记本电脑上测试了五种草稿/目标模型组合，从三个层面验证了分布等价性。结果表明投机解码确实保持输出分布，但加速效果受限于硬件和模型组合。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.
